scenarios.name: Controlled command-line launch
scenarios.purpose: Validate command execution mapping from process creation telemetry.
success_criteria: process_name or command_line contains a command interpreter or script host
parent process information is available when possible
safety_notes: Use only in an approved isolated VM snapshot.
Treat telemetry as observed evidence only when collected from an approved isolated VM.

scenarios.name: Loopback network callback fixture
scenarios.purpose: Validate network behavior mapping from DNS, socket, or HTTP telemetry.
success_criteria: destination host, IP, port, or URL is captured
process_name or pid links the network event to the fixture process when possible
safety_notes: Use isolated or sink-only networking for real VM validation.
Do not allow unrestricted internet callbacks.

scenarios.name: File write fixture
scenarios.purpose: Validate file operation mapping from file create telemetry.
success_criteria: file_create or file_write event path contains C:\Telemetry\fixtures\file_write_marker.txt
process_name identifies powershell.exe or the script host
safety_notes: Writes one marker file only.
Run cleanup after validation.

scenarios.name: Registry Run key fixture
scenarios.purpose: Validate registry persistence mapping from registry set/create telemetry.
success_criteria: registry key path contains CurrentVersion\Run or CurrentVersion\RunOnce
value data records the executable or command when available
safety_notes: Use an isolated VM snapshot before any real registry write validation.
Restore VM state after real validation.

scenarios.name: Controlled process-access fixture
scenarios.purpose: Validate process-injection mapping from benign cross-process access telemetry.
scenarios.sample_type: benign process-access fixture or simulated telemetry
success_criteria: A source process opens a handle to a short-lived child process
Telemetry records process_access or equivalent cross-process access evidence
No remote code payload is injected
safety_notes: The fixture uses benign process access only.
Treat this as telemetry-pipeline validation, not as a real injection payload.

scenarios.name: Credential access query fixture
scenarios.purpose: Validate credential-access mapping from lsass-related command or telemetry without reading credentials.
scenarios.sample_type: benign query fixture or simulated telemetry
success_criteria: command_line references lsass or credential-access terminology
no credential material is read, dumped, or exported
safety_notes: This fixture must not dump credentials.
It validates mapping logic using benign lsass query wording.

scenarios.name: Anti-analysis environment probe fixture
scenarios.purpose: Validate anti-analysis mapping from debugger, VM, sandbox, or monitoring-tool probes.
success_criteria: command_line or registry query references vmware, virtualbox, procmon, wireshark, or debugger checks
fixture writes only marker evidence and does not modify security controls
safety_notes: Environment probing is read-only except for marker files.
Do not treat fixture telemetry as malware behavior.

scenarios.name: Anti-VM and sandbox evasion probe fixture
scenarios.purpose: Validate anti-VM, sandbox, debugger, idle-time, disk, adapter, and timezone probe mapping.
success_criteria: command_line or marker content references vmware, virtualbox, sandbox, debugger, PhysicalDrive, adapter, timezone, or idle-time checks
fixture performs read-only environment queries and writes only marker evidence
safety_notes: Environment probing is read-only except for marker files.
Do not disable security tools or modify host protections.

scenarios.name: System discovery fixture
scenarios.purpose: Validate system information discovery mapping from host, OS, memory, CPU, and architecture queries.
success_criteria: command_line or marker content references GetComputerName, GetSystemInfo, GlobalMemoryStatus, systeminfo, or OS discovery fields
no system configuration is modified
safety_notes: Read-only local discovery only.
Writes only a marker file under C:\Telemetry\fixtures.

scenarios.name: Privilege discovery fixture
scenarios.purpose: Validate privilege and permission discovery mapping from token, group, and privilege-name queries.
success_criteria: command_line or marker content references whoami /priv, LookupPrivilegeValue, SeDebugPrivilege, AdjustTokenPrivileges, or token privilege fields
fixture does not enable or escalate privileges
safety_notes: Privilege checks are read-only.
Do not use the fixture to change token privileges.

scenarios.name: File and directory discovery fixture
scenarios.purpose: Validate file and directory discovery mapping from benign enumeration telemetry.
success_criteria: command_line or telemetry references dir, Get-ChildItem, FindFirstFile, FindNextFile, or directory enumeration
enumeration stays under C:\Telemetry\fixtures or another approved lab path
safety_notes: Read-only enumeration of lab fixture paths.
Run cleanup after validation.

scenarios.name: Trace setting modification fixture
scenarios.purpose: Validate trace-setting registry modification mapping from controlled Microsoft Tracing key writes.
success_criteria: registry path contains Microsoft\Tracing and EnableFileTracing, ConsoleTracingMask, or AutoFileTracing
fixture cleans up the controlled test value
safety_notes: Use isolated VM snapshots for registry-write validation.
Do not modify defensive telemetry service settings.

scenarios.name: Static packer and obfuscation fixture
scenarios.purpose: Validate static high-entropy or encoded-blob mapping for packed or obfuscated artifacts.
success_criteria: static analysis sees encoded or high-entropy content
packer_or_obfuscation remains candidate-only unless unpacking evidence exists
safety_notes: This is primarily a static validation fixture.
No payload is downloaded or executed.

scenarios.name: Windows service creation fixture
scenarios.purpose: Validate service creation mapping from service telemetry.
success_criteria: service name is SFPValidationService
service is deleted during cleanup
service binary path points to a benign command marker
safety_notes: Requires an isolated VM snapshot and an account allowed to create services.
Run cleanup after validation and restore the VM snapshot.

scenarios.name: Scheduled task fixture
scenarios.purpose: Validate scheduled task evidence collection and behavior mapping.
success_criteria: task name is SFPValidationTask
process creation or TaskScheduler telemetry references the fixture
safety_notes: Current-user task only.
Run cleanup after validation.
